CMake toolchain configuration for STM32F4 microcontroller using arm-none-eabi-gcc.

# Purpose
This configuration file is used to set up the build environment for a project targeting an ARM-based microcontroller, specifically the STM32F4 series. It specifies the system name as `MCU` and the processor architecture as `armv7l`. The file sets common architecture flags under `FLAGS_COMMON_ARCH` to configure the compiler for little-endian, thumb mode, and specific ARM Cortex-M4 features, including floating-point support. The `CMAKE_FORCE_C_COMPILER` command enforces the use of the `arm-none-eabi-gcc` compiler, which is a GNU compiler for ARM embedded processors. This setup ensures that the build process is tailored for the specified microcontroller architecture and processor.
